case.title: COMMISSIONER OF INTERNAL REVENUE v. SCHLUDE

opinion.h: COMMISSIONER OF INTERNAL REVENUE v. SCHLUDE, 367 U.S. 911 (1961)
COMMISSIONER OF INTERNAL REVENUE v. SCHLUDE ET UX. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE EIGHTH CIRCUIT. No. 629. Decided June 19, 1961.
opinion.p: Certiorari granted; judgment vacated; and case remanded.
Solicitor General Rankin, Assistant Attorney General Rice, Harry Baum and George F. Lynch for petitioner.
Robert Ash and Carl F. Bauersfeld for respondents.

The petition for writ of certiorari is granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded for further consideration in the light of American Automobile Association v. United States, ante, p. 687.
367 U.S. 911 FAMILY FAIR, INC., ET AL. v. OHIO. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 754. Decided June 19, 1961.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS is of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
